app.name: A11yFred
app.tagline: Your AI-second helper for manual accessibility auditing and reporting.
common.sponsored_body: Receive ads from companies in the accessibility and developer communities. Your privacy is respected, no tracking, no profiles, no algorithms.
onboarding.heading: Welcome to A11yFred
onboarding.confirm_body: You have not reached the end of the tour. Close now and skip the rest?
onboarding.slide_1_body: Describe what you observed: a component name, symptom, or WCAG success criterion. Use the Platform and WCAG Level filters to narrow the corpus. Use Narrow Results to filter within what is already showing. Results are sorted by relevance, then by severity.
onboarding.slide_2_body: Use Rank Up/Down to rank results by importance. Star a finding to always show it first. Pin a finding to keep it at the top across all searches.
onboarding.slide_3_body: Open a finding to see its title, WCAG criterion, description, and suggested fix. Add a location prefix to the description (e.g. 'Global:'). Use AI Assist to revise any field using your notes, then copy fields individually or use Copy All to grab everything at once.
search.label: Describe what you observed
search.placeholder: e.g. modal close button, no accessible name
search.hint_submit: Type a description and press Search or Enter.
search.hint_platform_native_caveat: Native app coverage is more limited than web.
search.hint_ranking: Use Rank Up/Down to adjust how results sort. Star a result to always show it first.
search.hint_pin: Pin any result to keep it at the top across all views.
search.hint_syntax_prefix: Use +term to require a word and -term to exclude one (e.g.
search.hint_change_in: These options can be changed in
search.narrow_clear_and_reset_aria: Clear narrow filter and return to full search
search.view_all_confirm_pinned_one: 1 result is pinned and always shown.
search.view_all_confirm_pinned: {count} results are pinned and always shown.
search.view_all_confirm_starred_one: 1 is starred and will always show on top.
search.view_all_confirm_starred: {count} are starred and will always show on top.
search.view_all_confirm_archived_one: 1 is archived and will always show at the end of the list of results.
search.view_all_confirm_archived: {count} are archived and will always show at the end of the list of results.
search.view_all_confirm_body: This will show {count} results on this single page.
search.view_all_confirm_body_one: This will show 1 result on this single page.
results.sort_live_hint: Live Search is on. Results will sort immediately after entering or making a selection change.
results.summary_query: Showing results for '{query}', sorted by '{sort}', narrowed by '{narrow}', {platform}.
results.sort_smart_info: Smart sorting orders by Severity first, then by your personal ranking score. Starred items always appear at the top; Archived items always appear at the bottom. This is the default sort order.
results.sort_wcag_level_info: AAA criteria have selective support in this corpus. Not all AAA success criteria are represented, and some entries marked AAA may reflect best-practice guidance rather than strict conformance targets.
results.sort_popularity_info: In a future update, this will reflect aggregated global popularity based on user interactions across all accounts. For now, popularity is session-based and resets when you close the app.
results.rank_hint: Rank results up or down to adjust how they sort. Star to show first. Archive to hide. (Can be undone.)
results.unpin_from_search: This result is pinned. Unpin to include it in search results.
results.badge_filter_aria: Show all findings with this tag
results.copy_link_aria: Copy link to these search results
results.no_results_announce: No results for '{query}'. Try a component name, element type, or a different phrase.
results.no_results_body: Try a component name (e.g. 'button'), element type (e.g. 'input'), or a different phrase.
results.no_results_settings_hint: You can also change filters individually in
detail.discard_confirm_body: Opening a new finding will discard your collapsed panel and any unsaved changes.
detail.location_placeholder: e.g. Global: / Cart: / Product details pages:
detail.edit_lang_warning: Edits are session-only. Switching language or finding will discard them.
detail.include_fix_title_when_copied: Include Suggested Fix title when copied
detail.copy_all_aria: Copy description and suggested fix together
detail.copy_all_announce: Description and Suggested Fix copied to clipboard
detail.reset_all_fields_aria: Reset description and suggested fix to original values
detail.confirm_reset_body: You have made significant changes to this text. Resetting will remove all of your edits.
detail.nothing_to_copy_body: This field is empty. There is nothing to copy.
detail.ai_revision_hint: Select which fields to revise using the checkboxes above, add your notes here, and AI will rewrite those fields. To change the AI model or turn this off, go to
detail.finding_note_hint: Add context here: location, severity notes, or anything project-specific. Enable AI Assist in
detail.finding_note_hint_suffix: and it will rewrite based on your notes.
detail.ai_revision_placeholder: e.g. this is specific to mobile, element is a tooltip
detail.finding_note_placeholder: e.g. Critical severity, found throughout the Onboarding flow. Lower severity issues may surface once these are fixed.
detail.ai_revision_aria: Save AI instructions and revise selected fields
detail.agentic_mode_help: Only available with Claude (Anthropic). The AI searches past revision examples to match the tone and depth you have already established.
detail.agentic_mode_hint: Claude will search past examples from this corpus to match your established tone and technical depth.
detail.ai_revision_error_body: Something went wrong while revising. Press Rewrite to try again.
detail.ai_revision_error_invalid_key: Invalid or expired API key ({provider}). Double-check your key in Settings.
detail.ai_revision_error_rate_limit: Rate limit reached ({provider}). Wait a moment and try again.
detail.ai_revision_error_service_error: {provider} service error ({status}). Try again in a few seconds.
detail.ai_revision_error_network_error: No internet connection. Check your connection and try again.
help.take_tour_description: Replay the onboarding tour to revisit how A11yFred works.
help.step_1_body: Results appear as you type. Use the Platform filter in Settings to choose Web or Native App findings (most are web-focused). Use +term to require a word and -term to exclude one (e.g. keyboard +screen reader -wcag2.2).
help.step_2_body: Each result has Rank Up and Rank Down buttons to change how it ranks. Star a result to make it always appear first. Pin any result to keep it at the top across all searches.
help.step_3_body: Select the finding that best matches what you observed. Results are sorted by relevance first, then by severity.
help.step_4_body: Add a location prefix to the description (e.g. 'Global:' or 'Cart:'). If AI Assist is on, add your notes to the Revision Notes field and AI will rewrite the finding to match your context.
help.step_5_body: Copy the description and suggested fix into your spreadsheet or report. Use Copy All to grab both fields at once.
help.shortcuts_note: Shortcuts are only active when focus is on the result list. Use them to quickly navigate and rate findings without leaving the list.
about.what_body: This is a library of accessibility findings for manual audits. Search for what you observed, find a description that fits, and copy it into your report.
about.what_body_2_prefix: The same issues come up on almost every site. This tool keeps your descriptions consistent across reports so you are not writing from scratch each time. The library was built from real audit work, then refined with AI for plain language, consistency, accuracy, and fact-checking against
about.what_wcag: This data covers WCAG 2.2 Level A and AA, the standard used by most organizations and the basis for many legal accessibility requirements. A small number of Level AAA Success Criteria are also included where the accessibility community broadly considers the failure pattern significant.
about.feature_ai_body: Use AI to rewrite a finding based on your notes. Bring your own API key from Anthropic, OpenAI, or Google. It stays in your browser and is never sent to us. Set it up in
about.feature_agentic_body: If you use Claude (Anthropic), you can also enable Match Existing Style. The AI will search past revision examples to match the tone and depth you have already established, not just rewrite the current finding in isolation.
about.feature_languages_body: This library is available in over 50 languages including RTL support for right-to-left languages. I have a love and fascination for language, culture, and linguistics. Adding all of these languages was a side quest, part-experiment, part-rabbit hole, part-losing the plot for a bit. The translations were AI-generated and some are incomplete, so please bear that in mind. Mistakes are expected, especially for technical accessibility terms. Corrections welcome. Switch in
about.feature_pinning_label: Pinning, Starring, Ranking, Archiving, Filtering, Sorting Results
about.feature_pinning_body: Pin any finding to keep it at the top across all searches. Star a finding to always show it first in ranked results. Use Rank Up and Rank Down to fine-tune sort order within severity. Archive a finding to push it to the bottom and visually dim it. Filter by platform or WCAG version and level to narrow the corpus. Sort by severity, title, WCAG criterion, or Smart order which combines severity with your personal ranking.
about.feature_a11y_body: Built to WCAG 2.2. Includes focus management, live regions, full keyboard navigation, reduced motion support, and high-contrast color tokens.
about.sources_body: The base corpus was built from real audit work across dozens of manual audits. AI was used to improve language clarity, cross-entry consistency, accuracy, source citation formatting, and WCAG Success Criterion alignment. Each finding is written in plain language and tied to a specific WCAG Success Criterion where one applies.
about.examples_ath010_label: Control Label Describes Appearance, Not Purpose
settings.heading: Settings
settings.unsaved_body: You have unsaved changes. What would you like to do?
settings.party_confirm_body: Things are about to get very weird and possibly inaccessible.
settings.reduced_motion_note: Reduced motion is on. Animations like confetti are skipped. To re-enable them, turn off reduced motion in your OS accessibility settings.
settings.language_rhg_body_1: This translation was AI-generated and has not been reviewed by native Rohingya speakers.
settings.language_rhg_body_2: The Rohingya people have endured genocide and forced displacement. Please use this translation with care, and consider contributing corrections if you are able.
settings.language_changed_announce: Language: Updated, press Save button to confirm
settings.platform_all_desc: Show all results across web, native apps, and documents.
settings.wcag_filter_desc: Filter findings by WCAG Version and Conformance Level. Each version includes all findings from previous versions.
settings.personal_corpus_on: Your custom findings appear in search results.
settings.personal_corpus_off: Your custom findings are hidden from results.
settings.ranking_on: Ranking controls are visible on each result.
settings.ai_enable_desc: Revises Descriptions and/or Suggested Fix based on your AI Instructions.
settings.api_key_error: Error: API key is required when AI Assist is enabled.
settings.agentic_mode_desc: Only available with Claude (Anthropic). Searches past revision examples to write in your established tone and technical depth.
settings.pinned_results_desc: Items you have pinned appear above all search results.
settings.starred_results_desc: Starred items always rise to the top, in the order they were starred.
settings.archived_results_desc: Archived items always appear at the very bottom of results.
settings.rankings_desc: Up and down ranks you have given to results. The higher a result is ranked relative to others, the earlier it appears in search.
settings.privacy_body_1: Everything is stored locally in your browser using localStorage. Nothing is sent to us or any server. Stored data includes: theme, language, platform filter, Live Search setting, AI provider preference, API keys, finding ratings, pinned findings, per-finding notes, recent findings, custom findings, personal corpus edits, co-selection pairs, pending contribution suggestions, and your last opened finding.
settings.privacy_body_2: API keys are stored in your browser only and sent directly to the AI provider you have configured. We never see or receive them. You can remove them at any time from the AI settings below, or by clearing your browser's site data for this page.
settings.privacy_body_translations: UI translations and the base public corpus were translated using AI. Some translations are incomplete and errors are expected. No user-entered data, custom findings, or personal edits are ever sent for translation.
settings.reset_all_announce: All Settings Reset and Data Cleared.
footer.credit: Built by your digital neighbor, Mikey Ilagan
error.announce: Something went wrong. The finding library could not be loaded. Check your connection and try again.
error.heading: Something Went Wrong
error.body: The finding library could not be loaded. Check your connection and
notfound.heading: 404 Not Found
notfound.body: Often when you think you're at the end of something, you're at the beginning of something else.
party.announce_full: Party mode activated! The font has changed to Comic Sans. The colors have changed to a random bright color palette. Colorful confetti is now falling from the top of the screen in an assortment of colors and shapes. The mouse cursor is now a magic wand.
party.announce_reduced: Party mode activated! The font has changed to Comic Sans. The colors have changed to a random bright color palette. The mouse cursor is now a magic wand. Confetti was skipped because reduced motion is turned on.
